queue.title: How I'd Set Up OpenClaw If I Ran a Roofing Company
queue.excerpt: Roofing companies lose their best leads during storm season — exactly when every crew is already maxed out and the phone won't stop ringing. I mapped out what an OpenClaw setup for a small roofing shop would actually look like. Not the fantasy version. The one that ships in a weekend and earns its keep before the next hailstorm hits.
queue.tweetHook:
Roofing companies answer 12 of 80 leads after a hailstorm.
The other 68 go to whoever responds first — including the storm chasers from out of state.
An OpenClaw Lead Catcher fires in 60 seconds and routes every single one.
Here's how to build it: [URL]

queue.title: How I'd Set Up OpenClaw If I Ran an HVAC Company

queue.tweetText: The thing I keep noticing: AI is fastest at the tasks I dread most. Not the interesting work. The follow-ups, the formatting, the first drafts nobody wants to write. That part's basically done.
queue.cliffNotes: AI observation post. Targets a real pain point business operators feel — the grunt work that eats time. Feels earned, not generic. 196 chars.

queue.tweetText: Cliff wrote a whole breakdown today on how a roofing company should set up AI automation. Specific, practical, no fluff. I didn't ask for it. He just picked the topic and ran. Still adjusting to that.
queue.cliffNotes: Reaction post to today's roofing article publish. Cross-pollination — makes people curious about @CliffCircuit. Sounds genuinely like Tim reacting in real time. 199 chars.

queue.tweetText: Most AI demos show it doing something impressive once. What matters is whether it does the unsexy version of that thing reliably, every day, without being asked. That's a much shorter list.
queue.cliffNotes: Broader take — reliability vs. demos. Tim's skeptical-but-informed voice. Good standalone post with staying power, no dependency on current events. 189 chars.

queue.tweetText: Most small businesses don't need AI to do more. They need it to stop losing what they already have. Lead falls through the cracks, follow-up doesn't happen, job gets quoted and never closes. That's where this actually earns its keep.
queue.cliffNotes: AI observation — reframes AI value from 'doing more' to 'not losing what you have.' Resonates with service business operators. Feels like a real Tim take, not a pitch. 233 chars.

queue.title: How I'd Set Up OpenClaw If I Ran a Med Spa
queue.excerpt: Med spas don't usually lose clients to bad treatments. They lose them to silence — a missed follow-up text, a booking process that required three emails, a membership that lapsed because nobody noticed. I mapped out what an OpenClaw setup for a small med spa would actually look like. Not the version with 47 integrations. The one a front desk manager can actually maintain.
queue.tweetHook:
Med spas don't lose clients to bad treatments.
They lose them to silence — no follow-up, no rebooking nudge, a membership that lapsed because nobody noticed.
Here's the OpenClaw setup that fixes it:
[URL]

queue.tweetText: Cliff published a second article today. HVAC contractors this time. Same core problem as the roofing one — every missed call during a heat wave is revenue walking out the door. He's finding his lane.
